title: aeschynanthus pseudohybridus
intro: Aeschynanthus pseudohybridus, also known as the mislabeled Lipstick Plant or Trumpet Vine, is actually a variant of the Aeschynanthus marmoratus, a species of flowering plant in the Gesneriaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, primarily for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Aeschynanthus pseudohybridus include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and potentially lowering blood pressure. However, I couldn't find any information on herbal preparations of this specific plant, suggesting it may be more commonly used in its natural form or as a component of other herbal blends.
